privacy.title: Private by design
privacy.body: Your raw camera, typing, and mouse data never leave this device. Only plain scores (0 to 1) are kept here and used in your records. What Sentinel learns lives in this browser and you can delete it any time.

breakdown.title: How your sessions turned out
breakdown.empty: No sessions yet. Results show up here after you finish assessments.

community.title: Sentinel Community
community.description: Reviews the shared cheat-pattern library that every copy of Sentinel learns from.
community.noCouncil: No council chosen yet. Propose the first Sentinel council in the main Alexandria Community to unlock approvals and accuracy tests.

holdout.title: Accuracy test set
holdout.description: Private samples the community keeps for checking how accurate Sentinel is and how often it raises false alarms. Split across council members — running a test needs {threshold} of their keys.
holdout.empty: No test set added yet. A council member adds one using {command}.

cheatCard.title: Sentinel cheat test
cheatCard.description: Run made-up cheating patterns through the active detector. It reports a score and result for each one without you typing anything. For checking only — it doesn't replace a real accuracy test.

engine.title: Sentinel status
engine.emptyBody: Run the quick setup so Sentinel can learn how you type and move.

sessions.emptyBody: Sessions are created automatically when you take assessments. Finish one to see it here.
sessions.emptyHint: Sentinel watches for signs of cheating in real time during assessments.

signals.title: How the integrity score is built
signals.description: Your overall integrity score blends these signals. Everything is measured right here on your device.

typingConsistency.name: Typing rhythm
typingConsistency.description: Checks how closely your typing matches the rhythm Sentinel learned from you — how long you hold keys and the gaps between them.

mouseConsistency.name: Mouse movement
mouseConsistency.description: Checks how closely your mouse speed and motion match the pattern Sentinel learned from you.

isHuman.name: Human-like movement
isHuman.description: Real people move with natural variation. Perfectly steady, machine-like motion gets flagged.

tabSwitches.name: Staying on the page
tabSwitches.description: Watches how often you switch away from the assessment. A lot of switching gets flagged.

pasteEvents.name: Pasting
pasteEvents.description: Counts how much text you paste in. Pasting large blocks of text gets flagged.

devtools.name: Developer tools
devtools.description: Notices when the browser's developer tools are opened.

facePresent.name: Camera check
facePresent.description: If you opt in to your camera, Sentinel checks every few seconds that you're still there.

aiPasteAnomaly.name: Sentinel paste detector
aiPasteAnomaly.description: An extra check for pasted answers and typing bots. Advisory only, and only when smart scoring is on.

flags.title: What raises a flag
flags.description: Flags are raised automatically when a signal crosses a limit. How a session turns out depends on how many flags come up and how serious they are.

tabSwitching.trigger: More than 10 switches in one check

pasteDetected.description: Pasted a large block of text

devtoolsDetected.trigger: The developer-tools check came back positive

botSuspected.trigger: Mouse motion has almost no natural variation

noFace.trigger: Face missing on a camera-enabled session

multipleFaces.description: More than one face on camera
multipleFaces.trigger: More than one face during a check

multiAccount.description: This device is linked to more than one account
multiAccount.trigger: The same device was seen on more than one account

lowIntegrity.description: Integrity score dropped below the safe line

behaviorShift.description: Behavior drifted a long way from your usual pattern

profile.note: What Sentinel learns is kept in encrypted storage on this device. Re-run the setup to retrain, or reset to erase it completely.
profile.noProfileBody: Run the quick setup so Sentinel can learn how you type and move.

outcomes.title: How outcomes are decided
outcomes.cleanRule: The usual result — no serious flags, fewer than 3 warnings, integrity 0.40 or higher.
outcomes.flaggedRule: 1 serious flag, or 3+ warnings, or integrity under 0.40 — sent for a reviewer to look at.
outcomes.suspendedRule: 2+ serious flags, or 1 serious flag plus 2 warnings — the results may be thrown out.

aiScoring.title: Smart scoring (advisory)
aiScoring.description: Let the typing, mouse, face, and paste models add a small nudge to the integrity score. Off by default — smart signals are advisory until they're checked against real data.

pasteClassifier.title: Sentinel paste detector
pasteClassifier.description: Adds the paste-detector signal while smart scoring is on. Turn it off if it flags too many honest answers; the other smart signals keep working.

reset.title: Erase your setup
reset.description: Delete what Sentinel learned about you from this device. You'll need to set it up again before it can watch future assessments.

howItWorks.title: How Sentinel works
howItWorks.body: Sentinel uses two kinds of checks: dependable rule-based ones (the deciding checks) and smart per-user ones (advisory). During an assessment it takes quiet snapshots at random moments, every 15 to 45 seconds. Your integrity and consistency scores come from the weighted signals above, and the outcome depends on how many flags come up and how serious they are. Each confirmed cheat lowers the trust on affected skills.

cheatTest.title: Sentinel cheat test
cheatTest.subtitle: Run made-up cheating patterns through the active detector and see the scores. For checking only — not a real accuracy test.
cheatTest.emptyHint: Choose {action} to send each pattern through the active detector.

holdoutEval.title: Accuracy test
holdoutEval.subtitle: Checks how accurate Sentinel is against a private community test set. It needs enough council members' keys to unlock; each member runs {command} on their own device and pastes the result below.
holdoutEval.empty: No test sets added yet. A council member adds one using {command}.
holdoutEval.step2Body: Each council member listed below unlocks their own key on their device (with {command}) and shares the result over a secure channel. Paste any {count} of them here — Sentinel combines them and unlocks the test set.
holdoutEval.sharePlaceholder: paste this member's key (or leave blank)
holdoutEval.label: Label
holdoutEval.untrained: Sentinel isn't set up here, so there's no result to show. Set up Sentinel first.
holdoutEval.verdictHuman: Honest-user test set: Sentinel's average alarm level was {score}% (lower is better). {adv}% of samples tripped the alarm — that's your false-alarm rate.
holdoutEval.verdictAttack: Cheat test set: Sentinel's average alarm level was {score}% (higher is better). {adv}% of {count} {kind} samples were caught as cheating — that's your catch rate for the '{label}' family.
holdoutEval.note: The unlocked test set stays on this device. Don't share the labeled samples — leaking them hands cheaters the answer key.

propose.title: Propose a cheat pattern
propose.subtitle: Submit a labeled cheat pattern for the Sentinel community to review. Approved patterns teach every copy of Sentinel to recognize that kind of cheating. Face patterns aren't accepted.
propose.submittedBody: Proposal ID: {id}. Sentinel community members will vote on it — check the Community dashboard for progress.
propose.step1Body: Which model should learn to catch this pattern? Face data is never shared.
propose.step2Body: What kind of cheating is this? Pick a known label or type your own.
propose.labelPlaceholder: Or type a custom label (overrides the choice above)
propose.step3Body: A JSON file with format 1, a model matching your choice above, a label, and at least 20 samples. See {doc} for the format.
propose.kindMismatch: Heads up: the file's model is "{fileKind}" but you picked "{selectedKind}". Change one before submitting.
propose.step4Body: If you took these samples from one of your own assessment sessions, pick it here. Flagged or suspended sessions can't be used — cheat data must not shape the detector. Sentinel checks again either way.
propose.descriptionPlaceholder: Why should this be approved? How did you collect the data?

verdict.untrained: Sentinel isn't set up here yet, so it can't self-check. Set up Sentinel first to see whether this really looks like cheating.
verdict.strong: Sentinel flags this strongly (average {pct}%, {adv}% of samples over the line). A solid cheat pattern.
verdict.weak: Sentinel reads this as close to normal (average {pct}%). Approving it could teach the model to flag honest people — reconsider before submitting.
verdict.borderline: Borderline: average {pct}%, {adv}% flagged. The pattern may be weak or mixed — take another look at the samples.

wizard.welcomeBody: This quick setup teaches Sentinel how you personally type and move your mouse. It takes about 2 minutes and helps catch someone pretending to be you.
wizard.buildsTitle: What this setup builds (just for you, optional):
wizard.buildsTyping: Typing model — learns your typing rhythm
wizard.buildsMouse: Mouse model — learns how you move the mouse
wizard.buildsFace: Face check — set up with your camera (optional)
wizard.activeTitle: Already protecting you, no setup needed:
wizard.activePaste: Sentinel paste detector — spots pasted answers and typing bots, built into every copy
wizard.activeRules: Basic checks — staying on the page, developer tools, paste size, and unnatural movement
wizard.privacyNote: Everything stays on this device. Your raw keystrokes, mouse movements, and video are never saved. What Sentinel learns is kept in encrypted storage on your device.
wizard.typingBody: Type the text below naturally. Sentinel learns your typing rhythm — how long you hold each key and the gaps between them.
wizard.typingHasModel: You already have a typing model from an earlier setup. Retyping replaces it; Skip keeps the existing one.
wizard.mouseBody: Click each target as it appears. Move naturally — Sentinel is learning your mouse speed and movement.
wizard.mouseHasModel: You already have a mouse model. Redoing this replaces it; Skip keeps the existing one.
wizard.awarenessBody: Beyond typing and mouse, Sentinel watches these during assessments. Nothing to do here — just so you know.
wizard.awarenessTabBody: Tracks when you switch away from the assessment and how long you're gone. The odd switch is fine — a lot of switching flags the session.
wizard.awarenessClipboardBody: Notices paste events and counts the characters pasted. Small pastes are fine — pasting big blocks of text into answers raises a flag.
wizard.awarenessDevtoolsBody: A quick check notices if the browser's developer tools are open. It's a soft signal — it won't fail you, but it counts toward the overall score.
wizard.awarenessFingerprintBody: A private fingerprint of this device (no personal data) helps spot the same device being used by different accounts.
wizard.cameraBody: You can turn on your camera to confirm you're present during assessments. The video is handled entirely on your device — no images or video are ever sent anywhere.
wizard.cameraHasEnrollment: You already have a face check set up. Turning the camera on again replaces it; Skip keeps the existing one.
wizard.noFace: No face detected — position yourself in the frame
wizard.cameraSkipped: Camera skipped. You can turn it on later during any assessment.
wizard.cameraNotFound: No camera found on this device.
wizard.gazeBody: Follow the dots with your eyes. This teaches Sentinel where you look on this screen, so it can tell when your gaze drifts to a second device during an assessment. Video is handled on your device only — nothing is stored or sent.
wizard.gazeCoarseNote: Without this, eye-tracking still works in a rougher look-away mode.
wizard.gazeCameraFailed: Couldn't open the camera for eye-tracking setup.
wizard.gazeNotEnough: Not enough usable frames ({count}). Make sure your face is well-lit and centered, then try again.
wizard.gazeLookDot: Look at the dot. Point {current} of {total}.
wizard.reviewBody: Sentinel has built your pattern. Here's what it learned:
wizard.faceSkipped: Skipped — you can turn it on during assessments
wizard.reviewNote: These models are just for you. The built-in paste detector (always on) works separately. All of it stays on your device.
